claim: The remote:* registrar performs NO sender validation: remote:agentsSave writes the named-agent registry to disk and remote:connect spawns an outbound ssh child from ANY webContents sender - the write-capable-surface precedent (FINDING-SEC-002/0005, register-registry/register-orky-action) gates such surfaces on WindowManager.isKnownWindowSender
fix: registerRemote gains an optional isKnownWindowSender?: (sender) => boolean dep (the register-registry signature); when provided, every handler rejects unknown senders (structured refusal for handles, silent drop for fire-and-forget); register.ts threads wm.isKnownWindowSender. TEST-2255 (gate absent = open) survives; add a pinned vector for the gated rejection at the tests phase
resolution: Verified: registerRemote gained isKnownWindowSender?; every remote:* surface now rejects an unknown sender (agentsList/current return empty, agentsSave rejects, connect/disconnect drop) and register.ts threads wm.isKnownWindowSender. Pinned by TEST-2281 (gated rejection + known-sender still flows).

claim: CONV-011 violated twice for the same membership set: (a) the slice's pruneRemoteStates has ZERO production callers, so remoteStates grows one orphan entry per closed remote workspace for the session's life; (b) the manager's entries map never deletes an entry either, so currentStates()/remote:current serve ghost workspaces forever - and closeWorkspace calls disconnectRemote BEFORE teardownPanes, so the entry still holds panes at disconnect time and even a pane-less-forget rule would miss
fix: closeWorkspace: teardown panes FIRST, then disconnectRemote, then pruneRemoteStates scoped to the closed id; manager.disconnectWorkspace deletes the entry after the cancelled push when it tracks zero panes (the open-workspace banner keeps its state via the no-state 'Not connected yet' model). Pinned vectors at the tests phase
resolution: Verified both sides: manager.disconnectWorkspace deletes a pane-less entry (no ghost in currentStates); store.closeWorkspace now teardownPanes -> disconnectRemote -> pruneRemoteStates in that order. Pinned by TEST-2282 (pane-less forget vs pane-holding retain) + TEST-2283 (closeWorkspace order).

claim: movePaneToNewWorkspace creates the destination workspace BEFORE movePaneToWorkspace refuses a cross-home move, stranding an empty orphan workspace after the refusal toast
fix: guard-first: consult paneMoveRefusalReason(from.home, undefined) before newWorkspace and toast+return on refusal
resolution: Verified: movePaneToNewWorkspace consults paneMoveRefusalReason(from.home, undefined) and toasts+returns BEFORE newWorkspace, so a refused cross-home move strands no orphan. Pinned by TEST-2283 (guard precedes newWorkspace).

claim: seedRemoteStates carries a vestigial seedSettled flag (written by ingestRemoteState, consumed only by a dead 'void seedSettled' statement) - the actual push-beats-pull arbitration rides key presence, so the flag is dead state masquerading as a mechanism
fix: delete the flag and the void statement; the key-presence rule is the documented mechanism
resolution: Verified: the vestigial seedSettled flag and its dead 'void' statement are removed; push-beats-pull rides key presence (documented). Existing TEST-2258 still green.

claim: the watchers satisfy paneIsRemote's GateState parameter with a synthetic remoteStates: {} - type-satisfying but semantically hollow; if paneIsRemote ever reads remoteStates the callers silently lie
fix: narrow paneIsRemote's parameter to Pick<GateState,'workspaces'> so the call sites pass exactly what is read
resolution: Verified: paneIsRemote's parameter narrowed to Pick<GateState,'workspaces'>; the Usage/Orky watchers now pass exactly { workspaces }.

claim: remote:state is an app-global broadcast, so in a multi-window session EVERY window's slice toasts the same disconnect failure - duplicate uncoordinated toasts for one event (the CONV-038 class, in-app variant)
fix: state the coexistence policy: gate the toast on the ingesting window hosting the workspace (get().workspaces[next.workspaceId] present), or accept and document per-window toasts; defer to the followups doc if not fixed in-run

claim: Inbound agent evt frames (pty:data/status/cwd/exit) forward by pane id WITHOUT an entry.panes membership check - a compromised or buggy remote host can inject output/status/cwd or a spurious exit into ANY pane id it guesses, including LOCAL panes of other workspaces (the routed send delivers wherever the pane lives). Found by the codex cross-check (item 1), verified against onEvt
fix: guard every pane-scoped evt on entry.panes.has(id) (drop + one diagnostic for unknown ids); the connection may only speak about panes it owns. Fix breaks no frozen vector (TEST-2249 uses a tracked pane; TEST-2233's post-revocation check stays inert); dedicated pinned vector deferred to the followups doc (freeze already captured this cycle)
resolution: Verified: onEvt drops any pane-scoped evt whose id is not in entry.panes (one diagnostic), so a remote host can no longer inject into unowned/local pane ids. Existing TEST-2249 (tracked pane) stays green.

claim: OrkyWatcher's cleanup unwatches only when a pane loses its cwd - a pane that became remote while KEEPING a cwd would leave a live local .orky watch running against a remote path (unreachable in v1: homes never change post-create; latent for any future re-home). Found by the codex cross-check (item 5)
fix: mirror UsageWatcher's desired-set diff: derive desired = local panes with cwds, unwatch anything not in desired
resolution: Verified: OrkyWatcher rewritten to the UsageWatcher desired-set diff (desired = local panes with a cwd), so a pane leaving the desired set for ANY reason is unwatched.
